[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Brussels+International+Fantastic+Film+Festival%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Brussels+International+Fantastic+Film+Festival%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Brussels+International+Fantastic+Film+Festival%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Brussels+International+Fantastic+Film+Festival%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(December 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(**BIFFF**), previously named **Brussels International Festival of Fantastic Film** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Festival international du film fantastique de Bruxelles*, [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Internationaal Festival van de Fantastische Film van Brussel*) was created in 1983 as a venue for [horror](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film"), [thriller](/wiki/Thriller_(genre) "Thriller (genre)") and [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction_film "Science fiction film") films. It takes place in [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"), every year in March. Initially organized by Annie Bozzo, Gigi Etienne, Freddy Bozzo, Georges Delmote and Guy Delmote, it now has prizes in both feature-length and short films, and also hosts an international body-painting competition.

["Competitive Specialised Film Festivals"](http://www.fiapf.org/intfilmfestivals_2016_sites02.asp). *fiapf.org*. Retrieved 26 May 2016.`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: deprecated archival service ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_deprecated_archival_service "Category:CS1 maint: deprecated archival service"))
["Twilight of the Warriors: Walled In wins the Golden Raven at the 43rd BIFFF"](https://cineuropa.org/en/newsdetail/476596/). *Cineuropa - the best of european cinema*. 22 Apr

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Annual film festival for speculative fiction film held in Brussels, Belgium

For other film festivals in Brussels, see [Brussels Film Festival (disambiguation)](/wiki/Brussels_Film_Festival_(disambiguation) "Brussels Film Festival (disambiguation)").

| --- | --- |

Brussels International Fantastic Film Festival

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Location | [Brussels, Belgium](/wiki/Brussels,_Belgium "Brussels, Belgium") |
| Founded | 1983 |
| Most recent | 2024 |
| Language | International |
| Website | [bifff.net](http://bifff.net) |
| Current: [43rd Brussels International Fantastic Film Festival](/w/index.php?title=43rd_Brussels_International_Fantastic_Film_Festival&action=edit&redlink=1 "43rd Brussels International Fantastic Film Festival (page does not exist)") | |
| 44th  [42nd](/w/index.php?title=42nd_Brussels_International_Fantastic_Film_Festival&action=edit&redlink=1 "42nd Brussels International Fantastic Film Festival (page does not exist)") | |

The **Brussels International Fantastic Film Festival** (**BIFFF**), previously named **Brussels International Festival of Fantastic Film** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Festival international du film fantastique de Bruxelles*, [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Internationaal Festival van de Fantastische Film van Brussel*) was created in 1983 as a venue for [horror](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film"), [thriller](/wiki/Thriller_(genre) "Thriller (genre)") and [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction_film "Science fiction film") films. It takes place in [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"), every year in March. Initially organized by Annie Bozzo, Gigi Etienne, Freddy Bozzo, Georges Delmote and Guy Delmote, it now has prizes in both feature-length and short films, and also hosts an international body-painting competition.

The festival is accredited by the [FIAPF](/wiki/FIAPF "FIAPF") as a competitive specialised film festival.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Winners of the grand prize, the Golden Raven statuette, include *[Army of Darkness](/wiki/Army_of_Darkness "Army of Darkness")*,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] *[Radioactive Dreams](/wiki/Radioactive_Dreams "Radioactive Dreams")*,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] and *[Dog Soldiers](/wiki/Dog_Soldiers_(film) "Dog Soldiers (film)")*.
